name: Fenwick Tree (BIT)
def: A compact array structure giving O(log n) prefix-sum queries and point updates.
problem: Maintain running aggregates (sums, counts) over a mutable array with both fast update and query.
mechanism: Each index stores a partial sum over a range determined by its lowest set bit; queries and updates walk by adding/subtracting that bit.
benefits: O(log n) update and prefix query
limits: Range-update needs a second tree or trick
Less general than a segment tree
failures: Using it for non-invertible ops like max
whenNot: Non-invertible range queries (use a segment tree)
myths: "Same as a segment tree" — Fenwick is smaller/faster but less general
